465888: Small stocky swallow-like bird with sandy grey-brown upperparts and paler buff-grey underparts, lacking strong contrast. Short slightly forked tail showing small white spots near tip when spread; blackish underwing coverts. Dark eye, short black bill, dark legs.
afbtit2: Small tit with bright cobalt-blue crown, wings and tail, white face bordered by dark blue eye-stripe and collar. Yellow underparts, greenish-blue back. Short black bill, bluish-grey legs; rounded compact body.
afrcha1: Robust finch; male has slate-grey head and nape, warm pinkish-brown breast and back, white wing bars and white shoulder patch, greenish rump. Whitish belly, conical greyish bill, brownish legs.
alepar2: Large long-tailed green parakeet with massive red bill, deep red shoulder patch on wing coverts. Male shows narrow black chin stripe and pink-and-black neck collar. Very long graduated blue-green tail; grey legs.
alpacc1: Plump streaky sparrow-like bird; grey-brown head and breast, chestnut-streaked flanks, dark-and-white spotted throat. Brown streaked back, dark wings with white-tipped covert spots forming a wing-bar row. Yellow-based dark bill, reddish legs.
alpswi1: Large powerful swift with scythe-shaped wings; brown upperparts and brown breast-band separating a white throat from a white belly. Short forked tail, dark eye, tiny black bill, dark legs.
arcloo: Large slender diver; smooth pale grey crown and hindneck, black throat patch, bold black-and-white striped neck sides, white-spotted black back. White underparts, dagger-like grey bill held level, dark legs.
arcter: Slender pale grey tern with full black cap, short red bill (no black tip), very short red legs. Long translucent white wings with narrow dark trailing-edge tip; deeply forked long white tail streamers, grey underparts.
arcwar1: Small olive-green warbler with long pale yellowish supercilium reaching almost to nape, dark eyestripe. Whitish underparts faintly washed yellow, one faint pale wing bar. Spike-like bill with pale orange lower mandible, pinkish-orange legs.
armgul1: Medium large gull with white head and underparts, slate-grey back and wings, black wingtips with white spots. Distinctive short stubby yellow bill with broad black band before a red gonys spot; dark eye, yellow legs.
atlfly1: Striking black-and-white flycatcher; male glossy black above and pure white below, with large white wing patch, white forehead patch, and a white rump band. Small black bill, dark legs.
atlpuf: Stocky black-and-white auk with large triangular bill, ridged red-orange with bluish-grey base. Black crown and collar, broad white-grey face, white underparts. Orange legs and feet; upright dumpy posture.
audgul1: Elegant pale gull with white head and body, pale grey back, black wingtips. Distinctive dark coral-red bill with black band and yellow tip; dark eye with red orbital ring, dark grey-green legs. Long-winged slim build.
azwmag3: Slim long-tailed magpie with glossy black cap, pale grey-buff body, and pale grey throat. Bright azure-blue wings and very long blue tail. Black bill and legs; graceful elongated proportions.
bahgoo: Pale grey goose with white head crossed by two bold black bars on the nape, white face stripe down the neck sides. Pale grey body, orange-yellow bill, orange legs.
baicra1: Tiny crake with olive-brown black-streaked and white-flecked upperparts, slate-blue-grey face and breast, black-and-white barred flanks and undertail. Short greenish bill (no red), dull green legs; very small dumpy body.
balshe1: Medium shearwater with dull brown upperparts and dirty buff-brown underparts, smudgy under-tail and vent. Slender dark bill, pinkish legs; long narrow wings held stiff. Overall drab muddy brown coloration.
banswa: Small brown swallow-martin; plain grey-brown upperparts, white underparts crossed by a distinct brown breast-band. White throat, short notched tail, tiny black bill, dark legs. Smallest, plainest hirundine.
bargoo: Boldly patterned goose with creamy-white face, black crown, neck, breast and bill. Silvery-grey back barred black and white, white belly and flanks, black legs. Compact build.
barpar2: Plump partridge with grey-brown back and breast; greyish face bordered by a broad chestnut collar speckled with white spots. Greyish crown, barred chestnut-and-grey flanks. Red bill, red legs.
barpet: Small dark storm-petrel, sooty blackish-brown overall with a broad clean white rump band wrapping onto the flanks. Pale wing-covert bar, slightly forked tail; short black bill, black legs.
barswa: Glossy steel-blue above with dark chestnut-red forehead and throat, a blue-black breast-band, and creamy-white underparts. Very long deeply forked tail with thin streamers showing white spots. Black bill, dark legs.
barwar1: Large bulky grey warbler; grey-brown upperparts, pale underparts finely barred with dark crescents (scaly look). Pale yellow eye, two thin pale wing bars, white-cornered tail. Stout grey bill, greyish legs.
batgod: Large long-legged wader; breeding male with brick-red head, neck and underparts, mottled grey-brown back. Long slightly upturned bill pink at base, black tip. Tail finely barred, dark legs.
bbspet1: Blackish storm-petrel with white rump and white belly patch divided by a dark central line down the underbody. Long legs, dark webbed feet projecting past short forked tail; black bill.
bcbeat1: Slender green bee-eater; overall grass-green with rufous-buff throat, blue cheek stripe and forehead bordering black eye-mask. Coppery underwing, long central tail streamers. Long down-curved black bill, dark legs.
bcnher: Stocky short-necked heron with black crown and back, pale grey wings, white face and underparts. Two long white nape plumes; large red eye, stout dark bill, yellowish legs.
bearee1: Long-tailed tawny-cinnamon bird; male has blue-grey head with bold black moustache drooping from eye, black-and-white undertail. Warm rufous-buff body, very long graduated tail. Yellow-orange bill, dark legs.
bkbplo: Plump large plover; breeding adult silvery spangled grey-and-black above, black face, throat and belly bordered by white, white undertail. Black armpits, stout black bill, grey-black legs.
bkewhe1: Slim wheatear; sandy-buff crown and back, pale orange-buff breast, white belly. Black throat (or black mask) and black wings; white rump and tail with black inverted-T pattern. Black bill, black legs.
bkewhe2: Slim wheatear with creamy-white crown and back, black face-mask through eye and black wings. Pale buff breast fading to white belly; white rump and tail with black inverted-T. Black bill, black legs.
bkhgul: Small gull; breeding adult with chocolate-brown hood, white neck and underparts, pale grey back. White leading wing-edge, black wingtips. Dark red bill and dark red legs, narrow white eye-crescents.
bklkit: Medium gull with white head and body, pale grey back, and clean black wingtips with no white spots (dipped-in-ink look). Slender yellow bill, dark eye, short black legs.
bkskit1: Small pale falcon-like raptor; pearly grey above, white head and underparts, with bold black shoulder patches on the upperwing. Large forward-set red eyes, short black-tipped bill, yellow legs; long pointed wings.
bktgod: Tall elegant wader; breeding adult orange-rufous head, neck and breast, barred lower belly, grey-brown back. Long straight bill pink at base with dark tip; black tail-band over white rump, long dark legs.
bkwsti: Very long-legged slim wader; pure white body and head, glossy black back and wings. Extremely long bright pink-red legs, fine straight black needle bill, red eye.
blackc1: Plain grey warbler; male grey-brown above, paler grey below, with a neat glossy black cap to eye level. Greyer face and nape, no wing bars. Fine dark bill, grey legs.
blagro1: Large grouse; male glossy blue-black with a lyre-shaped curved tail, white undertail coverts forming a puffy white patch, white wing-bar. Bright red wattle over eye, dark bill, feathered dark legs.
blakit1: Medium brown raptor; dark rufous-brown overall with paler greyish head and faint pale wing patches. Long wings, distinctly shallow-forked tail. Yellow legs, dark-tipped yellow bill.
blared1: Small chat; male sooty-black face, throat and breast, dark slate-grey crown and back, blackish belly with whitish wing panel. Rusty-orange rump and tail sides. Fine black bill, black legs.
blasto1: Tall stork; glossy black head, neck, back and wings with green-purple iridescence, sharply contrasting white belly and undertail. Long straight bright red bill, long red legs, red eye-ring.
blawhe1: All-black wheatear; sooty black plumage overall with contrasting white rump, white undertail coverts, and white tail-base showing a black terminal band. Black bill, black legs.
blawoo1: Large crow-sized woodpecker; entirely black with a glaring pale yellow eye and a bright red crown (full red crown on male). Long neck, chisel pale ivory bill, grey legs.
blbsan1: Bulky sandgrouse; male sandy-buff above with fine dark spotting, grey head and throat with chestnut throat patch, narrow black breast-band above a black belly. Short greyish bill, feathered legs.
blhbun1: Bright bunting; male has glossy black head, lemon-yellow throat and underparts, and a rich rufous-chestnut back and rump. Plain unstreaked yellow belly, stout pale grey conical bill, pinkish legs.
blkfra: Striking gamebird; male glossy black with bold white cheek patches, a broad chestnut neck-collar, and white-spotted black flanks. Brown-barred wings and back, reddish bill, orange-red legs.
blkgui: Small black auk; sooty black overall with a large bold white oval wing patch. Bright scarlet-red mouth lining and legs; slender pointed black bill, dark eye.
blksco1: Bulky all-black sea duck; male entirely glossy black with a knob at the bill base; bill black with a yellow-orange patch on the upper ridge. Dark legs, pointed tail.
blkswa: Large all-black swan; sooty-black plumage with white flight feathers visible in the wing. Long curved neck, bright red bill with a white band near the tip, dark legs.
blkter: Small marsh tern; breeding adult slate-grey body with a black head and breast, dark grey wings, and white undertail coverts. Slightly forked grey tail, slender black bill, dark reddish legs.
blrwar1: Small plain warbler, uniform warm olive-brown above and pale buff-cream below, with a short faint pale supercilium and pale eye-ring giving a gentle expression. Long fine dark bill, dark legs, and a rounded tail; no wing bars or streaking.
blueth: Robin-shaped chat, warm brown above with a bold white supercilium; throat brilliant blue bordered below by black, then a rufous band, with a central red or white spot. Belly whitish; rusty-orange tail base flashes in flight, thin dark bill, slim dark legs.
blutit: Small compact tit with sky-blue crown, wings and tail, a white face crossed by a black eye-stripe and blue-black nape collar, and bright yellow underparts. Greenish back, short stubby black bill, bluish-grey legs.
blwpra1: Slim tern-like wader, warm brown above and on the breast with a black-bordered creamy throat patch, white belly, and forked tail. Diagnostic blackish underwing-coverts, short red-based dark bill, long dark legs.
bohwax: Plump pinkish-grey bird with a prominent backswept crest, black mask and bib, and a chestnut undertail. Wings show white and yellow markings with waxy red tips; tail tipped bright yellow, short dark bill, dark legs.
boneag2: Large eagle, dark brown above with a pale whitish breast and belly finely streaked dark, and a whitish nape patch. In flight long broad wings and a long grey barred tail with a broad dark terminal band; yellow legs and cere, dark hooked bill.
booeag1: Small compact eagle (pale morph) with creamy-white underparts and underwing-coverts contrasting dark flight feathers, brown upperparts with a pale wing panel. Square-tipped tail, small head, feathered legs yellow-toed, dark hooked bill.
boowar1: Tiny pale warbler, sandy grey-brown above and creamy-white below, with a short pale supercilium and dark eye-line, plus a faintly dark-bordered crown. Short fine bill with pale base, pale legs; small rounded body with short tail.
borowl: Small owl with a large square greyish-white facial disc bordered black, raised white-spotted eyebrows giving a surprised look, and yellow eyes. Chocolate-brown upperparts heavily spotted white, white underparts streaked brown; pale bill, feathered feet.
brambl: Finch with orange breast and shoulders, blackish head and back (mottled in winter), and a white belly. Bold white rump conspicuous in flight, dark wings with orange and white bars, stout pale or dark-tipped bill, pinkish legs.
brant: Small dark goose with black head, neck and breast, a small white neck collar patch, dark brownish-grey back, and pale flanks. White undertail and stern conspicuous, short black bill and black legs.
brbsan: Tiny short-legged sandpiper, dark brown above with whitish mantle stripes forming a split supercilium and pale-edged feathers, streaked breast, white belly. Long slightly downcurved black bill kinked at tip, short dark legs.
bripet: Tiny blackish storm-petrel with a square white rump patch and white underwing bar, otherwise sooty all over. Short rounded wings, square tail, small black bill with tubenose, black legs and feet.
brnowl: Pale heart-shaped white facial disc with dark eyes, golden-buff and grey upperparts finely speckled, and pure white underparts. Long white-feathered legs, dark-tipped pale bill, rounded wings; ghostly overall appearance.
brnrav1: Large all-dark corvid, glossy black with a bronzy-brown wash on the neck and nape visible in good light. Long heavy black bill, slim long-winged silhouette, wedge-shaped tail, black legs.
budger: Small slender parakeet, bright green below with a yellow face and head finely barred black on the nape and back, plus dark throat spots and a blue cheek patch. Long tapering blue-green tail, small grey-blue cere bill, greyish legs.
bulpet: Slim sooty-brown petrel, uniformly dark with a paler diagonal bar across the upperwing-coverts. Long pointed wings, a long pointed wedge-shaped tail held closed, short hooked black tubenose bill.
burthr: Slim thrush-sized chat, uniformly deep slate-blue overall with fine pale spangling, slightly darker wings and tail. Long thin blackish bill, dark legs; rounded head and upright posture.
cacgoo1: Small compact goose with black head and neck, a white chin-strap cheek patch, brown back and pale breast. Short stubby black bill and rounded head distinguish it; white rump band, dark tail, black legs.
callar1: Large bulky lark, streaky sandy-brown above and whitish below with a bold black patch on each side of the neck. Heavy pale conical bill, broad wings showing a white trailing edge in flight, pale legs, short white-cornered tail.
cangoo: Large goose with a long black neck and head bearing a broad white chin-strap cheek patch, brown back, and pale tan breast. White undertail and U-shaped rump band, black bill and black legs.
casgul2: Large pale grey-backed gull with white head and underparts, long slender pale-yellow bill, and a small dark eye giving a gentle look. Long legs pale pink to yellowish, black wingtips with white spots, sloping forehead and long neck.
caster1: Very large tern with a massive blood-red dagger bill, black cap, pale grey back and wings, and white underparts. Dusky underside to the primaries, slightly forked tail, short black legs.
categr1: Small stocky white egret with a short thick yellow-orange bill, hunched neck, and buff-orange plumes on crown, breast and back when breeding. Yellowish to dusky legs, rounded head; otherwise pure white.
cetwar1: Small rich rufous-brown warbler, warm chestnut-brown above and greyish-white below with greyer face and a short whitish supercilium. Broad rounded cocked tail, short rounded wings, fine dark bill, pinkish legs.
chifla1: Tall pinkish flamingo, pale rose-white body with brighter pink wing-coverts, and grey legs with contrasting bright red knees and feet. Strongly downbent bill pale with a black tip, long sinuous neck.
chiwig1: Striking duck with a glossy green-black head, white forecrown and cheeks, rich chestnut flanks, and a black-and-white scaled back. White wing patch, dark vermiculated breast, short blue-grey bill, grey legs.
cinvul1: Huge dark vulture, uniformly blackish-brown with a bare bluish-grey head ruffed with dark down and a pale horn-coloured bill base. In flight enormous broad rectangular wings and short wedge tail, pale feet.
cirbun1: Bunting with an olive-grey breast band and yellow underparts; male head has a black throat and eye-stripe with yellow supercilium and cheeks. Streaked chestnut back, rufous flanks streaked dark, grey rump, stout greyish bill.
citfin1: Small finch, bright yellow-green below and on the face with a soft grey nape and neck-sides, and an olive-green streaked back. Two pale wing bars, unstreaked yellowish underparts, short stubby grey bill, dark legs.
citwag: Slender wagtail with a bright lemon-yellow head and underparts, a black hindneck band, and a blue-grey back. Two bold white wing bars on dark wings, long black tail with white outer feathers, thin black bill, dark legs.
coatit2: Small tit with a glossy black cap and bib, white cheeks, and a diagnostic white nape patch. Olive-grey back, buff-grey underparts, two thin white wing bars, fine dark bill, bluish-grey legs.
cockat: Slim grey parrot with a tall pointed crest, white wing patches, and a long tapering grey tail. Face yellow with a round orange cheek patch; small grey hooked bill, grey legs.
cogwar1: Small skulking warbler, olive-brown above heavily streaked dark on crown, back and rump, with paler buff streaked breast. Faint pale supercilium, plain undertail, rounded graduated tail, fine pointed bill, pinkish legs.
colfly1: Black-and-white flycatcher; male glossy black above with a broad white collar around the neck, large white wing patch, white forehead, and pure white underparts. White rump, small flat black bill, dark legs.
colpra: Slim tern-like wader, sandy-brown above and on the breast with a black-bordered creamy throat patch, white belly, and deeply forked tail. Chestnut underwing-coverts, red-based dark bill, short dark legs.
combul2: Plain brownish bulbul, dusky grey-brown overall with a darker brown head, slightly paler throat, and whitish lower belly with yellow undertail-coverts. Slim slightly downcurved black bill, dark legs, longish tail.
combuz1: Medium raptor, variable brown above and below with a pale band across the lower breast and barred underparts. In flight broad rounded wings with dark wrist patches and dark trailing edge, barred tail, yellow cere and legs, hooked bill.
comcan: Small finch, soft greenish-yellow overall, streaked grey-green on the back, with a yellower face and breast and whitish belly. Two faint wing bars, short conical pinkish bill, pale legs.
comcha: Finch with a blue-grey crown and nape, pinkish-brown face and breast, and a greenish rump. Bold double white wing bars and white shoulder patch on dark wings, white outer tail feathers, conical grey bill, pinkish legs.
comchi1: Tiny plain warbler, dull olive-brown above and off-white washed buff below, with a faint pale supercilium and pale eye-ring. Fine dark bill, blackish legs distinguishing it; short rounded wings, frequently dipped tail.
comcra: Tall elegant grey crane with a black face and foreneck, a white stripe down the side of the neck, and a small red crown patch. Drooping bushy black-and-grey tail plumes, long dark legs, straight greyish bill.
comcuc: Slim grey bird with a long graduated white-spotted tail, grey head and breast, and white underparts finely barred grey. Pointed falcon-like wings, yellow eye-ring and feet, slightly downcurved dark bill with yellow base.
comeid: Large heavy sea-duck; male white above with a black crown, pale green nape, white breast washed pink, and black flanks, belly and tail. Wedge-shaped grey-green bill sloping into forehead, dark legs.
comgol: Compact diving duck; male with a glossy green-black rounded head, a round white spot before the golden-yellow eye, white body, and black back. Black-and-white wings, short dark bill, yellow-orange legs.
comgre: Tall pale grey-brown wader, greyish above mottled darker, with a white belly and streaked breast. Long slightly upturned bill grey with a dark tip, long greenish legs, white rump and lower back wedge in flight.
comhom1: Small martin, glossy blue-black above with a conspicuous square white rump, and pure white underparts. Short forked tail, broad-based pointed wings, tiny black bill, and white-feathered legs and feet.
comkin1: Small dumpy kingfisher with brilliant electric-blue upperparts and a turquoise back stripe, orange underparts, and orange ear patch behind a white throat. Long straight dagger bill, short red legs, large head, stubby tail.
comloo: Large diver, blackish-green head and neck with a white necklace of vertical stripes, a checkerboard black-and-white back, and white underparts. Heavy straight dagger-shaped black bill, red eye, low in water.
commer: Large slim sawbill; male with a glossy green-black head, a thin red hooked bill, white body washed pink, and a black back. Long low profile, dark wings with a large white patch, red legs.
commoo3: Medium dark slate-grey waterbird with brownish back, a bright red frontal shield and red bill tipped yellow, a broken white line along the flanks, white undertail coverts split by black, and long greenish-yellow legs with very long toes.
commur: Slim black-and-white auk with dark chocolate-brown head, neck and upperparts, clean white underparts, a long pointed black bill, thin white trailing edge to the wing, and dark legs set far back.
commyn: Stocky brown bird with glossy black head and throat, bare yellow skin patch behind the eye, yellow bill and legs, white wing patches and white tail tip conspicuous in flight, and a dark vinous-brown body.
comnig1: Plain warm rufous-brown bird with a brighter chestnut-red rounded tail, paler buff-grey underparts, faintly large dark eye, fine pale eye-ring, slim dark bill and pinkish legs; robin-like but larger and longer-tailed.
compea: Large iridescent blue-necked peacock with a fan-shaped crest of wire-tufts, glossy green-and-bronze train studded with blue-green eyespots, scaly bronze-green wing feathers, white face patches and grey legs.
compoc: Medium diving duck with rich chestnut-red head, black breast and rear, pale grey vermiculated body, a black bill crossed by a pale blue-grey band, and a red eye; sloping forehead profile.
comqua1: Tiny rotund streaky game-bird, sandy-brown above with cream streaks, buff underparts, a bold cream crown-stripe and supercilium, dark eyestripe, pale throat bordered by dark anchor marks, and pale legs.
comrav: Very large all-glossy-black corvid with purple-blue sheen, a massive heavy black bill, shaggy throat hackles, long wedge-shaped tail and broad fingered wings; stout black legs.
comred1: Medium grey-brown wader, streaked above and on the breast, with bright orange-red legs and a straight red-based black-tipped bill; in flight shows a broad white trailing wing-bar and white rump wedge.
comred2: Slim chat with blue-grey crown and back, black face and throat, white forehead, bright orange-red breast and flanks, and a rusty-orange rump and quivering tail; thin black bill and dark legs.
comros: Plump finch with rosy-red head, breast and rump in the male, brown wings and back, whitish belly, plain face with a large dark eye, two faint wing-bars and a stubby pale conical bill.
comsan: Small brown wader with olive-brown upperparts, clean white underparts forming a distinctive white wedge in front of the bent wing, fine breast streaking, a white eye-ring, pale straight bill and greenish legs; constantly teetering rear.
comshe: Large goose-like duck, mostly white with a dark glossy-green head, a broad chestnut breast-band, black scapular and belly stripes, a bright red bill with a knob, and pink legs.
comsni: Cryptic wader, richly mottled brown above with bold cream back-stripes, dark crown split by a pale central stripe, heavily barred flanks, a very long straight bill and short greenish legs.
comswi: All sooty-black aerial bird with a pale greyish throat patch, very long slender scythe-shaped swept-back wings, a short forked tail and a tiny black bill; streamlined cigar-shaped body.
comter: Slender pale-grey tern with a black cap, pale grey underparts, an orange-red bill tipped black, short red legs, long pointed wings and deeply forked white tail with long streamers.
comwax: Tiny greyish-brown waxbill finely barred all over, with a bright red bill and a broad red mask through the eye, a reddish belly patch, and a long dark tail; small and slim.
corbun1: Large plump dull bunting, streaky grey-brown above and on buff underparts, with dense dark streaking on the breast forming a central spot, a plain face, a pale stout conical bill and pinkish legs.
corcra: Buff-brown rail with grey face and breast, bold chestnut barring on the flanks, blackish streaking on the back, a short stout pale bill, pinkish legs, and bright rufous wing patches in flight.
corfin1: Small finch with a yellow-green face and breast, grey-brown nape and crown, streaked greenish-brown back, yellowish wing-bars and yellow wing-flash, and a pale conical bill; brighter and greyer-capped than a Citril Finch.
corplo: Small plump plover, sandy-brown above and white below, with a complete black breast-band, black mask and forehead, a white forehead and collar, an orange bill with black tip and orange-yellow legs.
corshe1: Large grey-brown shearwater with a pale-scaled grey-brown head and back blending into white underparts, a thick pale yellow bill with a dark tip, dark underwing margins and long broad-winged silhouette.
cowpig1: Large grey pigeon with a pinkish breast, a bold white patch on each side of the neck, a broad white wing-bar across the grey wing, an iridescent green-purple neck sheen, and a yellow-tipped pinkish bill.
crebun2: Bunting with a blue-grey head and breast, a rusty-orange throat and eye-ring, warm rufous-brown streaked back, pinkish belly and a pinkish conical bill; like Ortolan but with grey throat.
crelar1: Sandy buff-brown lark, streaked above and on the breast, with a long spiky upstanding crest, a longish slightly down-curved pale bill, pale buff underwing in flight, and a short tail with buff outer feathers.
cretit2: Small grey-brown tit with a tall pointed black-and-white speckled crest, whitish face outlined by black, a black bib and a curved black collar-mark, pale buff underparts and a short dark bill.
crwfin2: Stocky pale sandy-brown finch with a grey head, pinkish-red wash on the face and wings, bold crimson-pink wing patches and tail edges, and a pale yellowish conical bill.
cursan: Slim wader with a long down-curved black bill, in breeding plumage rich brick-red on the head, underparts and back which is also scaled black, and a white rump; black legs, longer-legged and longer-billed than Dunlin.
cypwar1: Small dark Sylvia-type warbler, slate-grey above, with a black head and heavy black scaling spotting on the white throat and breast, a red eye-ring and pale iris, and a long blackish white-edged tail.
cypwhe1: Small wheatear with black face, throat and wings contrasting with a white crown, nape and underparts, a black inverted-T tail pattern on white, and black legs; crisp black-and-white pied appearance.
dalpel1: Huge silvery-grey-white pelican with curly nape feathers, an orange-red gular pouch in breeding, a pale yellowish-grey bill, greyish legs, and pale grey feet; bulky with a massive bill.
darwar1: Small dark long-tailed warbler, slate-grey above, with a dark wine-red breast and throat speckled white, a red eye and red eye-ring, a cocked dark white-tipped tail, and a thin dark bill.
delpri1: Tiny pale sandy-grey prinia, faintly streaked above, with whitish underparts, a pale supercilium, a slim dark bill, pinkish legs and a long graduated tail tipped white; slender and long-tailed.
desfin2: Pale sandy-grey finch with a black-and-white wing pattern showing pink at the base of the flight feathers, a black bill, a black mask around the eye and a black chin, and a buff-grey body.
desspa1: Small sparrow with a grey head, a chestnut crown-side band and broad white supercilium, a black bib, yellow spots at the throat sides, chestnut back streaked black, and whitish underparts.
deswhe1: Pale sandy-buff wheatear with a black face and throat that join black wings, sandy crown and back, pale buff underparts, and an all-black tail (no white inverted-T) above a small white rump.
diadov1: Tiny pale blue-grey dove, brownish on the back and wings spangled with small white spots, a grey head and breast, a deep red eye-ring, a dark slender bill and a long tail; very small and delicate.
doveki: Very small chubby auk, black above and white below, with a tiny stubby black bill, a black head and breast, white belly and short neck; tubby compact shape with dark legs.
dunlin: Small wader with a black belly patch in breeding plumage, a rufous-and-black scaled back, streaked whitish breast, a slightly down-curved black bill, black legs and a faint white wing-bar; hunched posture.
dunnoc1: Small streaky brown bird with a blue-grey head and breast, brown black-streaked back, brownish flanks streaked dark, a thin pointed dark bill and pinkish-brown legs; sparrow-sized but slim-billed.
duplar1: Slim long-bodied lark, sandy grey-brown streaked above and on the breast, with a pale supercilium and eye-ring, a long thin slightly down-curved bill, a peaked crown and pale buff legs.
duswar: Small plain dark warbler, dull grey-brown above and buffish-white below with buff flanks, a long buff supercilium, a dark eyestripe, a fine dark bill and dark legs; lacks any wing-bars.
eabwar1: Small pale leaf-warbler, greenish-grey above with a yellowish-green rump and wing-panel, plain whitish underparts, a plain face with a faint pale eye-ring, a pale lower mandible and dark legs.
eaowar1: Small plain pale warbler, greyish-brown above and whitish below, with a faint pale supercilium, a longish dagger-like bill with an orange base, a pale eye-ring and pale legs; featureless and slim.
eaowar2: Largish robust Sylvia warbler, grey above with a dark grey-black hood, whitish underparts with a peach wash on the flanks, a pale staring yellow eye, a strong bill and white outer tail feathers.
eargre: Small grebe with a black head and neck, a fan of golden-yellow ear plumes spreading behind a red eye, a peaked crown, dark back, chestnut flanks and a thin slightly upturned dark bill.
easwar1: Small Sylvia warbler with a blue-grey head and back, a brick-orange throat and breast, a white moustachial stripe separating throat from face, white belly, a red eye-ring and white outer tail.
eaywag1: Slim long-tailed wagtail with a yellow throat and underparts, an olive-green back, a blue-grey head with a white supercilium, blackish wings with pale-edged coverts, and dark legs.
egygoo: Large pale buff-brown goose-like duck with a dark chocolate patch around each eye and on the breast, a chestnut-brown back, pink bill and legs, and bold white wing patches with green visible in flight.
egyvul1: Smallish vulture, mostly creamy-white with black flight feathers, a bare yellow-orange face, a shaggy white ruff and spiky nape, a thin yellow hooked bill and a wedge-shaped white tail.
elefal1: Slim long-winged falcon with long tail; dark slate-grey above, rusty-buff underparts with dark streaking, dark moustachial stripe on pale throat. Yellow cere and feet, dark wingtips; dark-morph birds are uniformly sooty.
ettwoo1: Small black-and-white woodpecker with barred black-and-white flanks, white-streaked black back, and no red; male shows a yellow crown patch. Black face with white stripes, dark bill, grey legs.
eubeat1: Slender, vividly coloured bird with chestnut crown and back, golden-yellow shoulders, turquoise-blue underparts, yellow throat bordered by black gorget, black eyestripe, long decurved black bill and projecting central tail spike.
eucdov: Pale sandy grey-buff dove with a thin black half-collar edged white on the hindneck, paler head, blackish primaries, broad white-tipped tail underside, dark bill and deep-pink legs.
eueowl1: Massive owl with mottled tawny-brown plumage streaked black, prominent ear tufts, large orange eyes, pale facial disc, finely barred underparts, powerful feathered legs and dark hooked bill.
eugori2: Brilliant golden-yellow bird with black wings and black tail with yellow corners, black lores, pinkish-red bill. Females and immatures are yellowish-green above with streaked pale underparts.
eugplo: Plump plover spangled gold and black above; breeding adult shows black face, throat and belly bordered by a broad white S-shaped band up the neck. Short black bill, grey legs, white underwing.
eugwoo2: Large green woodpecker, grass-green above, pale greenish-grey below, with bright yellow rump, red crown and nape, and black mask around white eye; male has red-centred black moustache. Greyish bill, grey legs.
euhbuz1: Buzzard-like raptor with small pigeon-like head, grey-brown above, variable pale-and-barred underparts and underwings, longish tail with two dark basal bands and one broad subterminal band. Yellow eyes, dark bill.
euhgul1: Large gull with pale grey back and wings, black wingtips with white spots, white head and underparts, stout yellow bill with red gonys spot, pale eye and pink legs.
eupfly1: Small flycatcher; breeding male glossy black above with white underparts, large white wing patch, white forehead spot and white outer tail. Black bill and legs; female is brown above with smaller wing patch.
eupowl1: Tiny, small-headed owl, greyish-brown spotted white above, whitish below with brown streaking, faint indistinct facial disc, yellow eyes, white eyebrows, and a relatively long barred tail. Yellowish bill.
euptit1: Tiny tit with a black mask through the eye, pale grey head, chestnut back, whitish underparts with rusty wash, and a fine sharply pointed grey bill.
eurbla: Medium thrush; male entirely glossy black with a bright orange-yellow bill and yellow eye-ring; female dark brown with faintly mottled paler throat and breast, duller bill. Dark legs.
eurbul: Plump finch with black cap, short thick black bill, grey back, black wings with a white wingbar, white rump and black tail; male has rosy-red cheeks and underparts, female greyish-buff below.
eurcoo: Sooty slate-black waterbird with a glossy black head, white frontal shield and white bill, red eye, and greenish legs with broadly lobed grey toes. Rounded plump body.
eurcrm1: Stocky brown martin, dusky grey-brown above, paler grey-brown below with a darker throat, slightly forked tail showing small white spots near the tip. Dark underwing coverts, small dark bill.
eurcur: Large brown wader streaked dark on buff, with a very long evenly downcurved bill, plain head without strong stripes, white rump and lower back, and long greyish legs.
eurdot: Plover with grey-brown upperparts, a broad white supercilium meeting in a V on the nape, grey breast bordered below by a narrow white line, chestnut belly and black lower belly patch. Short dark bill, yellowish legs.
eurgol1: Small finch with a bright red face, white cheeks and black-and-white head, warm buff-brown back, and black wings crossed by a broad bright yellow wingbar. Whitish underparts, pale pointed bill.
eurgre1: Stocky finch, olive-green above, yellowish-green below, with bright yellow flashes on the wing edges and at the tail base, grey on wings and face, and a stout pale conical bill. Pinkish legs.
eurgri1: Huge pale sandy-brown vulture with a whitish downy head and neck, ruff of pale feathers, contrasting blackish-brown flight feathers and tail, broad wings, and a heavy pale bill.
eurhob: Slim, scythe-winged falcon, slate-grey above, whitish underparts heavily streaked black, with a dark hood and bold black moustache on white cheeks, and rufous-red thighs and vent. Yellow cere and feet.
eurjac: Small compact crow, sooty black with a pale silvery-grey nape and ear-coverts, and a striking pale whitish-grey eye. Short black bill and black legs.
eurjay1: Pinkish-brown crow with a streaked black-and-white crown, black moustache, white rump, black tail, and a brilliant blue-and-black barred patch on the wing. Black bill, white throat.
eurkes: Slender falcon; male has a blue-grey head and tail with a black subterminal band, rufous back spotted black, and buff black-spotted underparts. Female rufous-brown barred above. Yellow cere and feet.
eurlin1: Small slim finch, chestnut-brown back, grey head, with a crimson forehead and rosy-red breast on breeding males, white-edged wing and tail feathers, and a small grey conical bill.
eurmag1: Unmistakable pied corvid, black head, breast and back, white belly and shoulders, very long iridescent green-blue graduated tail, and dark blue gloss on the wings. Black bill and legs.
eurnig1: Cryptic, intricately mottled grey-brown bird with a flat broad head, very large dark eyes, tiny bill with a wide gape, long wings and long tail; males show white spots near wingtips and tail corners.
eurnut2: Compact tit-like bird, blue-grey above, with a black eyestripe, white cheeks and throat, and warm buff-orange underparts becoming chestnut on the flanks. Strong straight dark bill, short tail.
eurnut3: Chocolate-brown crow boldly spangled with white spots over the body, with a plain dark cap, white undertail coverts, blackish wings and tail, and a long pointed dark bill.
euroys1: Bold black-and-white wader, black head, breast and upperparts, white belly, broad white wingbar and rump, long straight orange-red bill, red eye, and stout pinkish legs.
eurrob1: Small plump bird with an orange-red face and breast bordered by blue-grey, brown upperparts, whitish belly, large dark eyes, thin dark bill and a rounded posture on thin dark legs.
eurrol1: Crow-sized, brilliantly coloured bird with a turquoise-blue head and underparts, rich cinnamon-brown back, and bright azure-blue and black wings showing dark flight feathers in flight. Stout dark bill.
eursco1: Tiny grey owl with intricately vermiculated bark-like grey-brown plumage, small ear tufts, yellow eyes, fine dark streaking below, and a pale scapular line. Greyish bill, feathered legs.
eurser1: Tiny streaky finch, greenish-yellow with heavy dark streaking on back and flanks, a bright yellow forehead, face and breast, yellow rump, and a very short stubby dark bill.
eursha1: Slim glossy greenish-black cormorant-like seabird with a slender neck, steep forehead, thin dark bill with a yellow gape patch, and a short upturned crest on the breeding crown. Dark webbed feet.
eursis: Small streaked yellow-green finch with a black crown and chin (male), bright yellow face, breast and wingbars, dark-streaked flanks, yellow rump, and a fine pointed bill. Forked tail.
eurspa1: Small short-winged hawk with long yellow legs and yellow eyes; male slate-grey above with fine rusty barring below, female grey-brown above with grey barring below. Long barred tail.
eurspo1: Large all-white wading bird with a long flattened spatulate black bill tipped yellow, black legs, and a shaggy yellowish nuchal crest and yellow breast band in breeding plumage.
eursta: Glossy black starling with green-and-purple iridescence, spangled with pale buff spots, a pointed yellow bill (breeding) and reddish-pink legs. Short tail, triangular wings.
eurtre1: Small slender bird, mottled brown above streaked white and buff, with a fine pale supercilium, plain white underparts, a long thin downcurved bill, and a long stiff pointed tail used for climbing.
eurwar1: Plain warbler, warm brown above and buff-white below, with an unmarked face except a faint pale supercilium, a sloping forehead, longish pointed bill, and pinkish legs.
eurwar2: Olive-brown unstreaked warbler with a faintly mottled olive-grey breast band, plain face with weak supercilium, rounded tail, longish undertail coverts, and pinkish legs.
eurwig: Compact dabbling duck; male has a chestnut head with a creamy-yellow forehead stripe, pink-grey breast, grey body, white wing patch and a black-and-white stern. Blue-grey bill with black tip.
eurwoo: Plump, long-billed woodland wader, intricately marbled rufous-brown, buff and black for camouflage, with bold dark transverse bars across the crown, large high-set dark eyes, and short pinkish legs.
eurwry: Small cryptic woodpecker-relative, intricately barred and vermiculated grey-brown with a dark stripe down the nape and back, finely barred buff underparts, mottled bark-like pattern, and a short pointed bill.
eutdov: Small slim dove with a scaly chequered black-and-rufous back and wings, blue-grey head, pinkish breast, a striped black-and-white neck patch, and a black tail with a broad white tip. Dark bill, pink legs.
eutkne1: Cryptic sandy-brown wader streaked darker, with a large rounded head, very large staring yellow eyes, a short black-tipped yellow bill, pale wingbars, and long thick yellowish legs.
eutspa: Small sparrow with a chestnut-brown crown, white cheeks marked by a black ear-spot, a small black throat bib, brown-streaked back, pale underparts and a stout grey conical bill.
ferduc: Small diving duck, rich mahogany-chestnut head, neck and breast, dark brown back, white belly and undertail coverts, and a striking white iris; blue-grey bill with black tip, broad white wing-stripe in flight.
fieldf: Large thrush with blue-grey head and rump contrasting chestnut-brown back, blackish tail; warm orange-buff breast heavily spotted black, white belly, dark arrowhead spots on flanks; yellow bill with dark tip.
finwhe1: Pale wheatear with whitish crown and underparts, black face, throat and wings; broad black inverted-T pattern on white tail; sandy-buff mantle, black bill and legs.
firecr1: Tiny kinglet, bright olive-green above, whitish below; bold white supercilium over black eyestripe, white below eye; flame-orange crown stripe bordered black, bronze shoulder patch, two white wingbars; thin black bill.
fuwduc: Tawny-orange goose-like duck with long neck and legs; rich cinnamon head, neck and underparts, dark brown back scaled buff, creamy-white flank stripes and U-shaped white uppertail band; dark grey bill and legs.
gadwal: Grey dabbling duck, finely vermiculated grey body, scaled grey breast, black rear end; brown head, white speculum patch on wing; dark grey bill, dull yellow legs.
gargan: Small dabbling duck, brown head with bold broad white crescent stripe from eye to nape; mottled brown breast, grey flanks, pale grey-blue forewing; dark bill, grey legs.
garwar1: Plump featureless warbler, uniform olive-brown above, pale buff-grey below; rounded head with plain face, faint pale eye-ring, no wingbars or strong markings; stout grey bill, blue-grey legs.
gbbgul: Very large gull with massive yellow bill (red gonys spot), blackish-slate back and wings, white head, underparts and tail; pinkish legs, pale eye.
gbhgul2: Large pale gull, white body, pale grey back and wings with black wingtips; in breeding has black hood with white crescents above and below eye; stout yellow bill with black ring and red tip, yellow legs.
glagul: Large pale gull, pale grey back and wings with no black, white wingtips, white head and underparts; heavy yellow bill with red gonys spot, pale eye, pink legs.
gloibi: Slender dark wading bird, deep maroon-chestnut head, neck and body with green-bronze iridescent gloss on wings and back; long downcurved grey bill, dark legs.
gnwtea: Tiny dabbling duck, chestnut head with broad green eye-patch bordered cream, vertical white bar on grey flanks; grey vermiculated body, buff-edged black undertail, green-and-black speculum; dark bill, grey legs.
goldcr1: Tiny rounded warbler, olive-green above, pale buff below; large dark eye on plain face; yellow crown stripe (orange-centred) bordered black, two white wingbars; very thin black bill.
goleag: Huge dark brown eagle with golden-buff crown and nape, long broad wings, longish tail; pale patches at wing base in flight; massive hooked grey-and-yellow bill, fully feathered legs, yellow feet.
gragoo: Large bulky grey-brown goose, paler greyish forewing and pale-fringed feathers; pale head and neck, whitish belly; heavy orange bill, pink legs.
graher1: Tall grey heron, grey back and wings, white head and neck with black eyestripe extending to thin crest; black-streaked white foreneck, dagger-like yellow bill, long legs.
grapri1: Tiny slim warbler, pale sandy grey-brown above, whitish below; very long graduated tail with black-and-white tips; faint pale supercilium, fine dark bill, pinkish legs.
grcgre1: Slender long-necked grebe, brown-grey back, white face and underparts, long white neck; black double crest and chestnut-and-black ear tufts (frills) on face; long pink-and-dark dagger bill, red eye.
grebit1: Stocky bittern, warm golden-buff plumage heavily mottled and streaked black-brown; blackish crown, dark moustache; long greenish-yellow dagger bill, greenish legs.
grebus1: Huge heavy bustard, sandy-rufous back barred black, white belly; pale blue-grey head and neck, long white moustache-like throat whiskers in male; thick neck, stout pale bill, sturdy grey legs.
grecor: Large blackish waterbird glossed bronze-green, white chin and cheek patch, yellow bare facial skin; thick hooked grey bill, black legs; often shows white thigh patch in breeding.
greegr: Tall all-white heron, slender long neck, long black-and-yellow dagger bill (yellow in non-breeding); long blackish legs and feet, no plumes on head.
grefla3: Very tall pink-tinged wading bird, pale pink-white body with bright rose-pink wing coverts, black flight feathers; long S-curved neck, downward-kinked pink bill with black tip, very long pink legs.
gresca: Diving duck, glossy green-black head, black breast and rear, white flanks, pale grey vermiculated back; rounded head, broad pale blue-grey bill with black nail, yellow eye.
greshe: Large shearwater, dark brown-grey above with pale-scaled back, white below; blackish-brown cap sharply cut off from white cheeks and white nape collar, dark belly smudge, white uppertail band; long dark bill.
gresku1: Bulky brown skua, dark rufous-brown plumage streaked paler, with bold white wing-flashes at base of primaries; heavy hooked dark bill, dark legs.
gresni1: Stocky snipe, richly patterned brown back with buff stripes, barred buff underparts; bold white-tipped outer tail feathers, dark-streaked head with stripes; long straight dark bill, greenish legs.
gretit1: Bold tit, glossy black head with large white cheek patches, black throat and broad black central belly stripe; yellow underparts, olive-green back, blue-grey wings with white wingbar; black bill, grey legs.
grewar3: Small slim leaf warbler, plain greyish-green above, whitish below; long whitish supercilium, dark eyestripe, one thin pale wingbar; fine dark bill with pale base, dark legs.
grewhi1: Slim warbler with grey-brown head, rufous-edged wings, brown back, white throat contrasting buff-pink breast and white belly; pale eye-ring, white outer tail feathers; dark bill, pale brownish legs.
grgowl: Very large grey owl with huge rounded facial disc marked with concentric grey rings, no ear tufts; small yellow eyes, white bow-tie marking on throat; mottled grey-brown body, yellow bill.
grnsan: Medium sandpiper, very dark greenish-brown back finely speckled white, white belly; bold white rump and barred tail in flight, dark underwing; short white supercilium before eye, white eye-ring; dark bill, greenish legs.
grnwar1: Small leaf warbler, bright greenish above, clean whitish below with yellow wash on face and throat; long yellowish supercilium, dark eyestripe, two pale wingbars; fine bill with orange lower mandible, pale legs.
grnwoo3: Green woodpecker, olive-green back, yellowish-green rump, pale grey-green underparts; grey head with red crown, narrow black around eye, red-centred black moustache; pale eye, greenish bill.
grrwar1: Large bulky reed warbler, warm brown above, buff-white below with buffy flanks; broad pale supercilium, peaked crown, sturdy build; strong dark bill with pale base, grey legs.
grscuc1: Large slim cuckoo, grey-brown back and wings boldly spotted white, long white-edged tail; grey crested cap, pale buff face and throat, creamy-white underparts; longish dark bill.
grseag1: Large dark brown eagle, uniformly dark chocolate-brown plumage, slightly paler nape, broad rounded wings; pale U-shaped band on uppertail coverts, dark bill with yellow cere, feathered legs, yellow feet.
grswoo: Pied woodpecker, black back with large white shoulder patches, black-and-white barred wings; white underparts, bright red vent; black crown (red nape patch in male), white face with black moustache; grey bill.
grwpel1: Huge white pelican with pinkish-white plumage, black underwing flight feathers; large pinkish-yellow throat pouch, long pale bill, yellowish gular skin and forehead; pinkish legs.
grypar: Plump round partridge, grey breast and neck finely barred, brown-streaked back, chestnut-barred flanks; orange-buff face, dark horseshoe belly patch; short grey bill, greyish legs.
grywag: Slim long-tailed wagtail, blue-grey back and head, yellow rump and bright yellow underparts (vivid on undertail); white supercilium, white wingbar, blackish wings; black bill, pinkish legs.
gstlar1: Small pale lark, sandy-brown streaked back, plain whitish underparts with dark patch at side of neck; pale supercilium, peaked crown, short stubby pale bill; pale legs, white outer tail feathers.
gubter2: Stout pale tern, pale grey back and wings, white underparts; full black cap in breeding; thick gull-like all-black bill, long black legs, short forked tail.
gwfgoo: Grey-brown goose with white blaze around base of bill, black irregular barring on belly; barred brown back, white undertail; pink-orange bill, orange legs.
gyfwoo1: Green woodpecker, olive-green back, yellow-green rump, pale grey head and underparts; small red forehead patch, thin black moustache and dark lores; pale eye, slender grey bill.
gyhchi: Brownish tit, dull brown cap, large white cheeks, blackish bib; grey-brown back, buff flanks, whitish underparts; short dark bill, dark legs.
gyrfal: Large powerful falcon, pale grey-white plumage variably barred and spotted dark grey above, white below with dark streaking; broad pointed wings, yellow cere and feet, dark hooked bill, faint moustache.
harduc: Striking small duck, slate-blue body with bold white crescents and spots edged black, chestnut flanks; white face patch, white crown stripe; small dark bill, grey legs.
hawfin: Large bull-necked finch, warm orange-brown body, grey neck collar, black throat patch; massive conical silver-grey bill, black-and-white wings with blue gloss, white wing patch and white tail tip.
hazgro1: Small plump grouse with intricately marbled grey-brown plumage barred chestnut, black and white; male has a black throat patch bordered white, short crest, grey tail with broad black subterminal band, and a red eye-comb. Short dark bill, feathered legs.
hoocro1: Large crow with pale ash-grey body and contrasting glossy black head, throat, wings and tail. Stout black bill, black legs. Robust build with a square tail; the grey-and-black two-tone pattern is unmistakable.
hoomer: Small slim sawbill with a huge fan-shaped white crest bordered black, black head and back, white breast crossed by two black bars, and rich cinnamon flanks. Thin black serrated bill, yellow eye. Compact body, long stiff tail.
hoopoe: Pinkish-cinnamon body with a bold black-and-white barred back, wings and tail. Erectile fan crest is cinnamon tipped black-and-white. Long thin slightly down-curved blackish bill, short greyish legs. Broad rounded wings boldly barred black and white.
horgre: Small grebe in breeding dress: black head with golden-yellow ear-tufts fanning back from the red eye, chestnut neck and flanks, blackish back. Short straight black bill with pale tip, flat-crowned peaked head. Dark legs set far back.
horlar: Sandy-brown lark with a striking black-and-yellow face: yellow throat and forehead, black mask and breast-band, and small black 'horn' feather tufts on the crown. Pinkish-brown back, white belly, dark tail edged white. Thin dark bill.
houbun3: Small grey-headed bunting with fine blackish head streaking, rich rufous-chestnut back, wings and tail, and pale greyish underparts washed cinnamon. Short conical pinkish bill, pinkish legs. Plain face lacking strong markings; warm rusty body tones.
houspa: Stocky sparrow; male has grey crown, chestnut-brown nape and back streaked black, grey cheeks, black bib, and pale grey underparts. White wing-bar on chestnut wings. Stout conical black bill, pinkish-brown legs. Plain whitish-buff face.
humwar1: Tiny dull olive-grey warbler with whitish underparts, a long buff supercilium, dark eyestripe, and a single narrow pale wing-bar. Greyish crown with faint paler median stripe. Thin dark bill with pale base, dark legs. Compact, short-tailed.
ibechi2: Tiny olive-green warbler with yellowish supercilium and throat, greyer crown and nape, and whitish belly washed yellow. Fine dark bill, dark legs. Slim and active; very similar to Common Chiffchaff but slightly brighter yellow-and-green tones.
ibgshr1: Pale grey shrike with a bold black mask through the eye, white throat and pinkish-white underparts, black wings with a small white patch, and black tail edged white. Stout hooked black bill, black legs. Slender long-tailed silhouette.
ictwar1: Medium warbler with plain greyish-green upperparts and lemon-yellow underparts, a pale yellow supercilium, and a pale wing-panel on the closed wing. Peaked crown, long stout pinkish-based bill, blue-grey legs. Long primary projection, sleek build.
impeag1: Large dark-brown eagle with a contrasting pale golden-buff crown and nape, white shoulder braces (scapular spots), and a greyish tail with broad dark terminal band. Yellow cere and feet, dark hooked bill. Long broad wings, heavy build.
itaspa1: Sparrow with a chestnut crown and nape, clean white cheeks, black bib, grey rump, and pale whitish underparts. Chestnut back streaked black, white wing-bar. Stout conical bill, pinkish legs. Brighter, cleaner-faced than House Sparrow.
ivogul: Wholly pure white gull with no markings, a rounded dove-like head, and dark eye. Short bluish bill tipped yellow-orange, and short black legs. Compact pigeon-like body; the all-white plumage with black legs is diagnostic.
jacsni: Small short-billed snipe with dark glossy purple-and-green back showing bold creamy-buff stripes, dark crown lacking a central pale stripe, and a split supercilium. Streaked buff breast, white belly, wedge-shaped pointed tail. Yellowish legs, fairly short straight bill.
kenplo1: Small pale sandy-brown plover with white underparts and forehead, a thin black frontal bar and eye-patch, and incomplete dark side breast-patches (no full band). Rufous tinge to crown. Slender black bill, blackish or grey legs. Slim, large-eyed.
kineid: Large sea-duck; male has a pale blue-grey crown and nape, green wash on cheeks, an orange frontal shield with red bill, white breast and black body. Black back with white shoulder patch. Heavy wedge-shaped head profile, stocky build.
krunut1: Small compact nuthatch with blue-grey upperparts, a black eyestripe and cap-edge, whitish throat, and buffy underparts marked by a large rufous-chestnut patch on the upper breast. Straight pointed grey bill, short tail, sturdy grey legs.
lammer1: Huge slender vulture with a small head; pale creamy-orange head, neck and underparts contrasting with dark slate-grey wings and back. Black mask and bristly 'beard' below the bill, red eye-ring. Long narrow pointed wings and long wedge-shaped tail.
lanwar: Small skulking warbler with olive-brown upperparts boldly streaked blackish, including streaked rump and breast, and pale buff underparts with dark malar streaks. Faint pale supercilium, rounded tail. Fine dark bill, pinkish legs. Heavily streaked overall.
laplon: Streaky brown bunting-like bird; breeding male has a black face and throat bordered by a white-then-buff stripe, a chestnut-red nape, and white belly with streaked flanks. Brown back streaked black, yellowish bill. Long hind claw, short pinkish legs.
laudov1: Slim long-tailed dove with a pinkish head and breast, rufous-brown back, and a distinctive black-spotted rusty 'necklace' patch on the lower throat. Bluish-grey wing panels, white-tipped tail edges. Small dark bill, reddish legs. Soft warm tones.
lbbgul: Large gull with a slate-grey back and wings (black wingtips with small white spots), white head and underparts, and white tail. Yellow bill with red gonys spot, yellow legs, pale eye. Streamlined; the dark grey mantle with yellow legs is key.
lcspet: Small dark sooty-brown storm-petrel with a white rump (often with a faint grey central line), a pale brown bar across the upperwing coverts, and a forked tail. Slim black bill, black legs. Long angular wings; buoyant build.
legshr2: Pale grey shrike with a broad black mask extending across the forehead, white underparts washed pink, black wings with a white patch, and black tail edged white. Pale grey crown and back. Short stout hooked black bill, black legs.
leseag1: Medium brown eagle with fairly uniform pale greyish-brown plumage, slightly darker flight feathers and a paler head and upperwing coverts. Yellow cere and feet, dark hooked bill, dark eye. Broad rounded wings, short tail; rather plain plumage.
leskes1: Small slender falcon; male has an unmarked brick-red back, blue-grey head, blue-grey greater coverts on the wing, and pinkish-buff underparts lightly spotted. Blue-grey tail with black band. Pale (whitish) claws, yellow cere and feet, short hooked bill.
leswhi4: Small grey-and-white warbler with a grey crown, darker grey ear-coverts (mask-like), greyish-brown back, and clean white underparts. White throat, dark legs, short blackish bill. Compact and neat; the contrasting dark cheek patch is distinctive.
leswoo1: Tiny sparrow-sized woodpecker with black-and-white barred (ladder-back) upperparts, whitish underparts finely streaked, and a black-bordered white face. Male has a red crown; black nape. Short pointed greyish bill, greyish legs. Smallest pied woodpecker.
lesyel: Slim elegant wader with greyish-brown back finely spotted white, streaked neck and breast, white belly, and a square white rump. Long bright yellow legs, a straight thin dark bill about head-length. Slender neck, fine proportions.
levspa1: Small slim hawk; male has blue-grey upperparts, very pale whitish underparts with faint rufous barring, blackish wingtips, and a grey tail with dark bands. Dark eye (not yellow), yellow cere and legs. Pointed wingtips, slender build.
levwoo1: Green woodpecker with yellowish-green back, pale grey-green underparts faintly barred on the belly, and a greyish face. Red crown extends to the nape; black moustache. Greenish-yellow rump, dark-tipped bill, greyish legs. Long stout grey bill.
lirplo: Small plover with brown upperparts, white underparts, and a single complete black breast-band; black-and-white forehead pattern and a bright yellow eye-ring. Black bill, dull pinkish to flesh-coloured legs. Slim, large-headed; the yellow eye-ring is diagnostic.
litbit1: Tiny heron; male has a black crown and back, large buff wing-coverts forming a pale oval panel, and buff neck and underparts. Greenish-yellow dagger bill and yellow legs. Slim long-necked build; the contrasting black back and pale wing-patch stand out.
litbun: Small bunting with a chestnut crown bordered by black lateral stripes, chestnut ear-coverts outlined black, a pale eye-ring, and a whitish 'spot' behind the cheek. Whitish underparts streaked black on breast and flanks. Small dark conical bill, pinkish legs.
litcra1: Tiny slim crake; male has slate-grey face and underparts, olive-brown back streaked black with fine white spots, and barred buff-and-white undertail. Short green bill with a red spot at the base, green legs with long toes. Long tail held cocked.
litegr: Slender all-white heron with a long thin neck, slim black dagger bill, black legs and contrasting bright yellow feet. Fine drooping plumes on the nape, breast and back in breeding dress. Elegant, graceful build with sharp angular profile.
litgre1: Small dumpy grebe with dark brown upperparts, chestnut cheeks and neck, dark crown, and a pale yellowish-green patch at the gape base. Short stubby dark bill with pale tip, fluffy rounded rear. Dark legs set far back; rounded, almost tailless shape.
litgul: Smallest gull, very small and tern-like, with a jet-black hood (breeding), pale grey upperparts, white underparts washed pink, and rounded wings showing blackish underwings. Short dark-red bill, red legs. Buoyant, delicate build; no black wingtips above.
litowl1: Small flat-headed owl with greyish-brown upperparts boldly spotted white, whitish underparts streaked brown, and pale 'eyebrows' over fierce yellow eyes giving a frowning look. Short pale bill, feathered whitish legs. Squat, short-tailed, large-headed.
litshe1: Small black-and-white shearwater with blackish upperparts and clean white underparts, white face extending above the eye, and white undertail-coverts. Thin dark bill, bluish legs. Compact, fast-fluttering build; black-capped appearance with white surrounds.
litsti: Tiny short-billed stint; breeding adult has rufous-fringed black-centred back feathers forming pale 'V' braces, a rufous-washed streaked breast, white belly and throat. Fine straight black bill, black legs. Diminutive, large-headed, quick.
litter1: Tiny pale tern with a black cap and white forehead, pale grey back and wings, and white underparts. Yellow bill tipped black, orange-yellow legs. Narrow pointed wings with a dark leading edge to the outer primaries; slim, fast-looking build.
lobdow: Medium snipe-like wader with greyish-brown upperparts (rufous-edged in breeding), reddish underparts barred on flanks, and a very long straight dark bill. White wedge up the back, greenish legs. Stocky body, short neck; sewing-machine feeding profile.
loeowl: Slim upright owl with mottled grey-brown plumage, vertically streaked underparts, long close-set ear-tufts, and a rusty-orange facial disc with bright orange eyes. Dark X-pattern across the face. Hooked dark bill, feathered legs. Slender, elongated shape.
lolbuz1: Large pale buzzard with a sandy-buff head and breast, rufous-tinged underparts, an unmarked pale rusty tail, and dark belly patch. Pale wings with dark wingtips and a dark carpal patch. Yellow cere and feet, hooked dark bill. Long-winged, lanky build.
lotduc: Small sea-duck; breeding male is dark brown and white with a white face patch around the eye, dark cheek-patch, and very long slender pointed black tail-streamers. Pink band across the dark bill, blackish breast, white flanks. Compact, round-headed.
lotjae: Slender graceful skua with grey-brown upperparts, a neat black cap, pale yellowish neck-sides, and clean white breast shading to grey belly. Very long pointed central tail-streamers. Short dark bill, dark legs. Elegant, tern-like flight silhouette.
lottit1: Tiny fluffy ball-shaped tit with a very long narrow tail. Whitish head (or with black lateral crown-stripes), blackish back, pinkish scapulars and flanks, and whitish underparts. Tiny stubby black bill, dark legs. Round body, long thin tail.
lwfgoo: Small compact grey-brown goose with a large white forehead blaze extending above the eye, bold black belly blotches, pink bill, and orange legs; bright yellow eye-ring is diagnostic, and folded wingtips extend past the short tail.
mallar3: Drake has a glossy bottle-green head, white neck-ring, yellow bill, chestnut-brown breast, grey body, black rear with curled central tail feathers, and orange legs; blue speculum bordered white shows on the wing.
manduc: Ornate drake with orange flared 'sail' feathers on the back, purple breast with white stripes, golden-buff flanks, a green-and-white crested head with broad white eye-stripe, red bill, and orange legs.
manshe: Slim black-and-white tubenose shearwater; clean blackish-brown above and pure white below from chin to undertail, with a dark cap reaching below the eye, narrow stiff wings, and dark slender bill.
marsan: Elegant pale slender wader with very long greenish legs, a fine straight needle-thin black bill, grey-brown streaked upperparts, white face and underparts, and a white rump and back wedge in flight.
martea1: Pale sandy-grey duck with dense whitish-buff spotting all over, a dark smudge through the eye, a shaggy nape, dark grey bill, and a slight crest; plain wings lack a bright speculum.
martit2: Small plain brownish tit with a glossy black cap and small neat black bib, dull buff-white cheeks, warm brown-grey upperparts, pale buffy underparts, a short dark bill, and dark legs.
marwar1: Small slim uniformly slate-grey warbler, slightly paler below, with a red orbital eye-ring, dark legs, and a long graduated dark tail often cocked; reddish iris and fine dark bill.
marwar3: Plain unstreaked warbler with warm olive-brown upperparts, pale buff-tinged whitish underparts, a faint pale supercilium, rounded head, pinkish legs, and a fine pointed bill; very similar to Reed Warbler.
masshr1: Slim long-tailed shrike with black crown, back and face mask, white forehead and large white supercilium, white throat and belly, rich orange flanks, white wing and tail patches, and a slender black bill.
meapip1: Small streaky brown pipit, olive-brown above with heavy dark streaking, whitish underparts boldly streaked on breast and flanks, a thin pale eye-ring, pink legs with a very long hind claw, and fine bill.
medgul1: Pale gull with an all-white wingtips, pale grey back, jet-black hood with broken white eye-crescents, bright blood-red drooping bill, and red legs; clean white underparts.
medshe1: Medium shearwater, brownish-black above and dull whitish below with dusky smudging on flanks and undertail; less clean than Manx, with a dark cap and slim dark bill, stiff narrow wings.
melwar1: Plain greenish-grey warbler, olive-toned above and pale lemon-yellow below, with a yellowish face and faint supercilium, short rounded wings lacking a pale panel, pinkish-brown legs, and a broad pale-based bill.
merlin: Small compact falcon; male slate-blue above with a rusty-streaked buff breast, faint dark moustache, and a broad black subterminal tail band; short pointed wings, yellow legs, dark-tipped yellow-based bill.
mewgul: Medium gull with a gentle rounded head, medium-grey back, white head and underparts, black wingtips with large white spots ('mirrors'), a plain greenish-yellow bill, dark eye, and yellow-green legs.
misthr1: Large pale grey-brown thrush with whitish underparts heavily marked with bold round black spots, greyish upperparts, white wing-bar edges, white-cornered tail, an upright stance, and pale belly; dark bill, pinkish legs.
miswoo1: Pied woodpecker with a full bright-red crown (no black border), black-and-white barred back with white shoulder patches, white face, pinkish-flushed lower belly, faint flank streaking, and a pale dark-tipped bill.
monhar1: Slender pale-grey harrier with long narrow wings, black wingtips and a single black bar across the upperwing, pale grey underparts with rusty streaks on belly and underwing; yellow eyes and legs.
monpar1: Bright green parakeet with a pale grey forehead, cheeks and scaly-grey breast, green crown and back, blue-tinged flight feathers, a long green tail, a pinkish-horn hooked bill, and grey legs.
mouchi2: Drab grey-brown leaf warbler with greyish upperparts, whitish underparts, a long whitish supercilium, dark eye-stripe, fine dark bill and blackish legs; lacks green and yellow tones, very plain.
moured1: Striking small chat; male slaty-black above with a bold white supercilium curving onto the nape, a large white wing patch, and bright rufous-orange underparts and rump; black tail, dark legs and bill.
mouwar1: Small rich warbler with rufous-brown upperparts, a bold white supercilium contrasting with a blackish crown and eye-stripe, white throat, warm buff flanks, rufous rump, and a fine dark bill; often cocks its tail.
mouwhe2: Pied wheatear; black face, throat, wings and back contrasting sharply with white crown, nape and underparts, buff-tinged vent, and a white rump with black inverted-T tail pattern; black bill and legs.
mstlar1: Small pale sandy short-toed lark, greyish-buff above with fine streaking, whitish below with light breast streaking, a pale supercilium, stubby pale finch-like bill, and pale pinkish legs; plain face.
musduc: Large heavy blackish duck with glossy greenish-black plumage, bold white wing patches, and extensive red warty bare skin (caruncles) around the face and bill base; dark legs, broad body, short legs.
mutswa: Very large all-white swan with a long gracefully S-curved neck, an orange bill with a prominent black basal knob, black facial skin and lores, and black legs; wings often arched over the back.
namdov1: Tiny long-tailed dove; male pale grey-brown with a black face, throat and breast, white belly, chestnut wing patches in flight, a very long pointed tail, and a red-and-yellow bill.
nohowl: Medium hawk-like owl with a long tail, brown-and-white finely barred underparts, white-spotted brown upperparts, a pale facial disc bordered by bold black sideburns, yellow eyes, and a yellowish bill.
norful: Stocky grey-and-white gull-like tubenose with a pale grey back and wings, white head and underparts, a thick neck, dark eye-smudge, and a short stout yellowish bill with prominent tube-nostrils.
norgan: Large white seabird with long narrow black-tipped wings, a buff-yellow wash on the head and nape, a long pointed pale-grey dagger bill, pale grey legs, and a slender pointed tail; cigar-shaped body.
norgos1: Large powerful hawk; slate-grey above, finely grey-barred white below, with a dark crown and broad white supercilium over a fierce orange-red eye, broad rounded wings, a long barred tail, and yellow legs.
norhar1: Slim grey-and-white harrier; male pale ash-grey above with black wingtips, white underparts and rump, long wings and tail; rounded head, yellow eyes and legs, low-set wings held in a V.
norlap: Black-and-white plover with iridescent green-glossed black upperparts, a long wispy black crest, black breast-band, white face and belly, orange-buff undertail, broad rounded wings, and dark reddish legs.
norpin: Elegant slender duck; drake has a chocolate-brown head, white breast extending as a stripe up the neck, grey body, black-and-cream rear, and very long pointed central tail feathers; blue-grey bill.
norsho: Drake has a huge spatulate black bill, glossy green head, white breast, chestnut flanks and belly, black-and-white back, and a pale-blue forewing patch; orange legs; front-heavy low-floating profile.
norshr1: Large pale grey shrike, soft grey above and white below, with a bold black face mask, black wings showing a white patch, a long black white-edged tail, and a hooked black bill.
norwhe: Slim chat; male blue-grey above, buff-white below, with a bold black face mask, white supercilium, black wings, and a white rump with a black inverted-T tail pattern; black bill and legs.
oltwar1: Large pale grey warbler, greyish above and whitish below, with a long heavy spike-like pinkish bill, a pale wing panel, a faint pale eye-ring, upright stance, and bluish-grey legs; warbler with a shrike-like bill.
ortbun1: Bunting with an olive-grey head and breast, a pale yellow throat and eye-ring (spectacle), rusty-buff belly, streaked brown back, a pinkish bill, and pinkish legs; subtle but distinctive head pattern.
ortdov: Large turtle-dove with a heavily black-and-rufous scaled back and wings, a pinkish-grey breast, black-and-blue-grey barred neck patch, a grey rump, dark tail with grey tip, and pinkish-red legs.
osprey: Large fish-eagle; dark brown above and white below, with a white head crossed by a broad dark eye-stripe through the eye, a dark wrist patch on long crooked wings, yellow eyes, and grey feet.
padwar1: Small warm-brown reed warbler with rusty-tinged upperparts and rump, a prominent whitish supercilium bordered above by a dark line, pale buff underparts, a short fine bill, and a rounded often-cocked tail.
palhar1: Slim pale harrier; male very pale silvery-grey above and white below with a small black wedge at the wingtip, very long narrow wings, a slender body, white rump, and yellow eyes and legs.
palroc1: Plain pale sandy-grey finch with unstreaked greyish-brown upperparts, pale whitish underparts, a stout conical pale bill, faint pale wing edgings, large dark eye, and pinkish legs; very nondescript.
palsun2: Tiny sunbird; male glossy metallic blue-green over head, throat and back, with a blackish belly and a small reddish-orange breast band, a long thin decurved black bill, and dark legs.
palswi3: Slim brown swift, paler sandy-brown overall than Common Swift, with a larger pale throat patch, a darker eye-mask, paler underparts, a broad blunt head, scythe-shaped wings, and a forked tail.
palwar5: Tiny bright leaf warbler, green above with a bold lemon-yellow rump band, two strong yellow wing-bars, a broad yellow central crown-stripe and supercilium, dark eye-stripe, and a fine dark bill.
parcro2: Bulky crossbill with a very large deep parrot-like crossed bill; male brick-red overall with browner wings, paler belly and rump, a thick neck and large head, and dark legs.
parjae: Slim dark-and-pale seabird with pointed central tail feathers projecting; pale-morph adult has a dark cap, yellowish neck, white underparts with a faint breast-band, dark upperparts, white wing flashes, and a small bill.
pecsan: Medium sandpiper with brown scaly upperparts and a sharply demarcated, densely streaked buff breast ending in a clean line against white belly. Faint pale supercilium, slightly drooping dark bill with paler base, and dull yellow-green legs.
perfal: Powerful falcon with slate blue-grey back and wings, pale whitish underparts finely barred grey-black, and a distinctive dark hooded head with broad black moustachial sausage on white cheeks. Yellow cere and eye-ring, yellow feet, dark hooked bill, pointed wings.
pieavo1: Elegant white wader boldly patterned with black cap, nape and wing bars on the white body. Long slender upturned black bill, long bluish-grey legs, and a graceful slim-necked shape.
piekin1: Crested black-and-white kingfisher speckled and barred all over; white underparts with one or two black breast bands, black mask and crest streaked white. Long straight black dagger bill, blackish legs, large head.
pifgoo: Compact grey-brown goose with a darker chocolate-brown head and neck, pale-fringed back giving a barred look, and pale grey forewing. Short dark bill with a pink central band, and bright pink legs and feet.
pingro: Plump large finch with a stubby curved black bill; male rich rosy-red on head, breast and rump with grey flanks and belly, blackish wings showing two white wing bars. Dark eye, dark legs, long notched tail.
pitsan1: Sandgrouse with a white belly, chestnut-bordered creamy breast band, finely vermiculated greenish-buff upperparts, and very long needle-like central tail feathers. Small pigeon-like head, short grey bill, feathered legs.
pomjae: Bulky dark skua, pale morph with blackish cap, white underparts, dark brown back and broad breast band, white wing flash. Diagnostic long blunt central tail feathers twisted into a spoon, heavy bill, dark legs.
purher1: Slender dark heron with a long sinuous neck; slate-grey back, rich rufous-chestnut neck striped with black lines, and dark purplish-maroon underparts. Long dagger-like yellowish bill, snaky head, very long yellowish legs.
pursan: Dark stocky sandpiper, slate-grey upperparts with purplish gloss and pale feather fringes, grey-streaked breast fading to white belly. Slightly drooping dark bill with orange-yellow base, and orange-yellow legs.
purswa: Large bulky rail in deep purple-blue plumage with turquoise sheen on throat and breast. Massive triangular red bill and frontal shield, red eye, long thick red legs and huge red feet, white undertail flashed in tail.
pygcor2: Small slim cormorant, dark brown-black plumage with a small rounded head and a notably long stiff tail. Short stubby yellowish bill, dark legs; overall daintier and longer-tailed than larger cormorants.
radacc2: Small grey-brown accentor with plain warm-brown back, buff underparts, and a striking pale buff-white supercilium contrasting with a dark eyestripe and greyish crown. Thin dark sparrow-like bill, pinkish legs.
razorb: Black-and-white auk with a glossy black head and upperparts, pure white underparts, and a thin white line running from eye to the bill base. Deep flat-sided black bill crossed by a vertical white line, black legs.
rebcho1: All-black crow with a glossy purplish sheen, broad rounded wings with splayed fingered primaries, and a square tail. Long curved bright red bill and bright red legs are the key marks.
rebfly: Tiny flycatcher with plain grey-brown upperparts, whitish underparts, and a peachy-orange throat patch bordered greyish. Bold white patches at the base of the blackish tail, white eye-ring, thin dark bill, dark legs.
rebgoo1: Small strikingly patterned goose, mostly black with bold white-bordered chestnut-red cheeks, throat and breast. White flank stripe and undertail, short stubby dark bill, black legs; compact rounded body.
rebmer: Slim diving duck; male has a dark glossy-green spiky-crested head, white neck collar, rusty-streaked breast band, grey flanks and black-and-white back. Thin serrated red bill, red eye, reddish legs.
rebshr1: Compact shrike; male has a blue-grey crown and nape, broad black mask through the eye, rich chestnut back and wings, and pinkish-white underparts. Black tail edged white, hooked black bill, dark legs.
recpoc: Large diving duck; male has a rounded rusty-orange head, black breast and stern, white flanks, and pale brown back. Bright coral-red bill, red eye, pinkish-red legs.
redava: Tiny finch; breeding male deep crimson-red overall, browner on wings and belly, spangled with many small white spots on flanks and wings. Stubby bright red bill, dark tail, pinkish legs.
redcro: Chunky finch with a unique crossed-tipped bill; male brick-red to orange-red overall, brightest on rump, with darker brown wings and tail. Female olive-grey-green. Dark legs, large head, short forked tail.
redkit1: Large graceful raptor with rich rufous-chestnut body and underwings, a pale grey streaked head, and a long deeply forked rusty tail. Wings show bold white patches under the black-tipped primaries; yellow legs, yellow-based hooked bill.
redkno: Stocky medium wader; breeding adult has brick-red face, neck and underparts, with grey, black and chestnut scaled upperparts. Straight medium black bill, short greenish-grey legs, dumpy rounded shape.
redpha1: Plump phalarope; breeding female brick-red below with a black crown, white face patch and dark grey-streaked back. Short stout yellow bill tipped black, lobed dark legs.
redpol1: Tiny streaky brown finch with a crimson-red forehead patch, small black chin bib, and a pinkish wash on the breast of males. Buff-and-brown streaked back, two pale wing bars, tiny yellowish bill, dark legs.
redwin: Small dark thrush with olive-brown upperparts, whitish underparts boldly streaked dark on breast and flanks. Striking creamy supercilium and rusty-red flanks and underwing, yellowish-based dark bill, pale legs.
